reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom spots in Tohoku; tool returns Akita general spots (stone circles, lakes, museums, volcanoes). No cherry blossom or sakura-specific entities. Wikidata records are well-structured with heritage_designations and qids. Data is accurate but off-topic for the query intent.
missing_entities: Kakunodate cherry blossoms (角館), Kitakami Tenshochi (北上展勝地), Hirosaki Castle (弘前城) — classic Tohoku sakura destinations
improvement_hint: Add sakura/cherry blossom category tagging so get_spots can filter by seasonal attraction type; or expose keyword search within prefecture endpoint.

reasoning: Query asks for sake breweries in Niigata; get_local_specialty returns MAFF GI food items (edamame, carrots, lotus root) — no sake breweries. The tool is wrong for this query type (breweries are not METI traditional crafts or MAFF GI). Data returned is accurate and well-structured but completely mismatched to intent.
missing_entities: Niigata sake breweries (e.g. Hakkaisan, Koshi no Kanbai, Kubota) — none present
improvement_hint: Add a brewery/distillery entity type or expose get_spots with lodging_type=brewery; alternatively map sake GI registrations (Niigata sake has GI) into the specialty dataset.

reasoning: Query is about ski resorts with hot springs in Hakuba; tool returns municipal_scrape nav/admin pages (観光課, tourism index pages) plus a lake and an art museum. No ski resort or onsen entities. Municipal scrape items lack heritage_designations, prominence_score, and kinds fields.
missing_entities: Hakuba 47, Happo One ski resort, Hakuba Goryu ski area, Mimizuku no yu onsen
improvement_hint: Add kinds tagging for ski_resort and onsen to Hakuba municipality scrape; filter low-quality admin nav pages (quality_score<0.5) from top results.

reasoning: Query asks for autumn foliage spots in Kansai; Kyoto results are highly relevant and prominent (Nijojo, Toji, Nishi Honganji, Ninnaji, Saiho-ji). All 4-language heritage sites with designations ranked first. Schema excellent: qid, url, coordinates, multilingual names, heritage_designations_labels all present.
missing_entities: Arashiyama, Tofuku-ji (top Kyoto koyo spots) not visible in truncated portion but likely present given 50-item limit
improvement_hint: Add seasonal_tags or koyo/hanami category flags so agents can filter specifically for foliage; Osaka/Nara cross-prefecture Kansai coverage would strengthen recall.

reasoning: Query asks for famous cherry blossom spots in Kansai; Nara results include Kasuga Taisha (famous for sakura) and other shrines. Municipal scrape items have generic category names (神社・仏閣) as the spot name rather than the actual attraction name, reducing usefulness. Yoshino (best Kansai sakura) not seen.
missing_entities: 吉野山 (Yoshino, top Kansai cherry blossom), Nara Park sakura
improvement_hint: Municipal scrape items should use specific landmark name not category string as primary name field; add sakura seasonal tagging for better query alignment.

reasoning: Query asks for stargazing spots on Okinawa remote islands; tool returns general Okinawa spots dominated by UNESCO castles on main island (Shuri Castle, Tamaudun, Nakagusuku). No remote island or stargazing entities. Kumejima town appears but without stargazing context. Schema is good for what's returned.
missing_entities: Iriomote Island, Taketomi Island, Miyako Island (all known for dark skies and stargazing)
improvement_hint: Add remote_island and stargazing/dark_sky category tags; expose island filtering within prefecture endpoint to surface離島 entities separately.

reasoning: Query asks for rural Hokkaido onsen accommodation; get_hotels returns large Sapporo city hotels (ANA Holiday Inn, ANA Crowne Plaza) ranked first. Rural onsen ryokan are not prominent. Some ski apartment entries (Full Circle, Gondola Chalet) in Niseko area appear but no onsen ryokan type flagged. No lodging_type=onsen_ryokan in visible results.
missing_entities: Noboribetsu Daiichi Takimotokan, Jozankei onsen ryokan, Sounkyo onsen hotels
improvement_hint: Expose onsen_ryokan lodging_type filter in get_hotels; add rural/urban flag or municipality-level filtering to deprioritize city center hotels for rural queries.

reasoning: Query asks for Seto Inland Sea islands; tool returns Kagawa general spots (Ritsurin Garden, Marugame Castle, Kotohira-gu). These are on mainland Kagawa, not islands. Naoshima or Shodoshima not visible in truncated 4000-char response. Heritage_designations and multilingual labels present and good.
missing_entities: Naoshima (直島), Shodoshima (小豆島), Teshima (豊島) — iconic Seto islands
improvement_hint: Add island entity type/kinds tag; expose municipality filtering for island municipalities (e.g. Naoshima-cho) to surface island-specific spots.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima; response returns 3 METI craft items including 阿波正藍しじら織 (Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave, authentic indigo weaving), 大谷焼 (ceramics), 阿波和紙 (washi). Primary indigo craft present and accurately described. Source URLs, designation dates, production areas all present. Only gap is no workshop/experiential venue data.
improvement_hint: Complement METI craft designations with workshop/experience venue data from municipal scrape; craft-experience spots would better serve 'workshops' intent.

reasoning: Query asks for shukubo (temple lodging) on Koyasan; get_hotels(Wakayama) returns Tanabe hotels, guest houses, onsen business hotels — none are Koyasan shukubo. No shukubo lodging_type exists. Major mismatch between query and data layer capability. OSM-only sources lack website/phone for many entries.
missing_entities: Ekoin shukubo, Fukuchiin shukubo, Rengejo-in shukubo — Koyasan temple lodging
improvement_hint: Add shukubo as a lodging_type; ingest Koyasan Shukubo Association data or filter by Koyasan municipality to surface temple accommodation entities.

reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto; search_area(武家屋敷) returns 82 matches with good diversity: Chiran (Kagoshima, heritage designated), Aizu Bukeyashiki (Fukushima), Kanazawa Nagamachi (Ishikawa via municipal scrape), Matsue Buke Yashiki (Shimane). Heritage_designations on Chiran. QIDs present. Chiran ranked prominently at score 0.85.
missing_entities: Kakunodate (角館) samurai district in Akita — not visible in top results
improvement_hint: Kakunodate should rank higher given its prominence; verify prominence_score for Q11545906 (Kakunodate Bukeyashiki) is set correctly.

reasoning: Query asks for summer festivals in Akita; response returns 3 intangible heritage items plus national_heritage section with UNESCO entries. Kanto Festival (秋田竿燈まつり) — Japan's most famous Akita summer festival — not present. Items returned are religious/ritual, not summer festival focused. Indonesian translation present and correct. UNESCO ids and source_urls all present.
missing_entities: 秋田竿燈まつり (Akita Kanto Festival) — most famous Akita summer festival, also UNESCO listed
wrong_facts: Early date (1705-01-01) on Tsuchizaki festival is origin year not event date — potentially confusing but not wrong
improvement_hint: Akita Kanto Festival should appear; verify it is in the bunka_intangible dataset and linked to Akita prefecture code correctly.

reasoning: Query asks for small fishing port towns in San'in region; Shimane spots include Iwami Ginzan (UNESCO), Matsue Castle, Lake Shinji, Izumo Taisha — all prominent but none are fishing port towns. However, for a prefecture-level query this is expected behavior. Izumo Taisha visible. Heritage designations and multilingual names present and correct.
missing_entities: Saburo fishing village, Oki Islands fishing ports — small harbor town entities
improvement_hint: Add kinds=fishing_port or harbor_town tag; expose coastal/fishing community entities from municipal scrape alongside landmark wikidata results.

reasoning: Query asks about washi papermaking in Gifu; response returns 8 craft items including Mino Washi (美濃和紙) — the primary washi of Gifu — along with Mino Ware ceramics, Hida Shunkei lacquerware. Primary entity present and described accurately. METI designation, source URLs, production areas all correct. Only gap is no interactive/experience venue data.
improvement_hint: Mino Washi experience workshops (美濃和紙の里会館) would enrich the response; consider linking craft designations to associated museum/workshop venues from municipal scrape.

reasoning: Query asks for fishing villages in Noto Peninsula; get_spots(Ishikawa) returns Kanazawa city attractions (Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, National Crafts Museum) ranked top. Noto-specific fishing village entities not visible. Nanao Castle appears (in Noto) but no coastal village/fishing port entities. Good schema but wrong geographic coverage for the query.
missing_entities: Wajima (輪島), Suzu (珠洲), Noto fishing villages, Senmaida (千枚田) terraced rice fields
improvement_hint: Add municipality filtering or sub-region tagging for Noto Peninsula; deprioritize Kanazawa city results when query implies peninsula/rural intent.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori; results dominated by Tottori Sakyu content (municipal scrape pages for Tottori tourism site). Some non-Tottori results appear (Hokkaido midden sites, Tokyo park) but these are shell middens/parks tagged as 砂, not actual sand dunes. Actual lesser-known dunes (Nakatajima, Uchimizushima) absent.
missing_entities: Nakatajima Sand Dunes (中田島砂丘, Shizuoka), Sarubetsu Gensei Kaen (Hokkaido)
wrong_facts: 高砂貝塚 is a shell midden, not a sand dune — returned due to kanji overlap but semantically wrong
improvement_hint: Add entity type=sand_dune to wikidata ingest; filter municipal scrape by relevance to query intent rather than broad keyword match.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional crafts in San'in (Shimane) in Chinese; response returns 4 METI craft items: Iwami Yaki ceramics, Sekishu Washi paper, Unshuu Soroban abacus, Izumo stone lanterns. All are authentic Shimane traditional crafts, accurately translated into Chinese, with METI source URLs. Complete and well-structured.
improvement_hint: Add Tottori crafts for full San'in region coverage (query mentions 山陰 which includes both Shimane and Tottori); consider cross-prefecture regional grouping.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields beyond Furano in Hokkaido; get_spots(Hokkaido) returns Matsumae Castle, Goryokaku, Showa-Shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower — all prominent heritage landmarks but none are lavender fields. Furano Farm Tomita or Biei lavender farms not visible. General Hokkaido spots dominate. Heritage metadata is good for what's returned.
missing_entities: Farm Tomita (Furano), Shikisai no Oka (Biei), Kamifurano lavender farms
improvement_hint: Add seasonal/flower attraction category (lavender, sunflower) as kinds tag to surface flower-farm entities from municipal scrape; Biei municipal pages likely contain this data.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional towns in Kyushu (kape = cape/traditional town in Filipino, likely historic townscape intent); Oita results show Usa Jingu, Beppu Hells, Oka Castle, Usuki Stone Buddhas. These are notable Oita attractions but not specifically traditional townscape districts. Usuki (臼杵) old town could qualify but response focuses on castle/shrine. No machiya/historic district entities.
missing_entities: Usuki historic townscape, Hita Mameda-machi historic district (大分県日田市豆田町)
improvement_hint: Add historic_district/traditional_townscape as kinds tag; Hita Mameda-machi is an important preservation district in Oita and should surface for this query type.

reasoning: Query asks for historic farmhouse (kominka) accommodation in Hokuriku; get_hotels(Ishikawa) returns student housing, ANA Crowne Plaza, generic guest houses. No kominka/machiya lodging type exists. First result is unnamed student housing. Hotels lack website/phone for many entries. No traditional accommodation entities visible.
missing_entities: Kayabuki kominka stays, Noto machiya accommodation, Hakusan area kominka guesthouses
improvement_hint: Add kominka/machiya as lodging_type; ingest traditional farmhouse stay data from Noto/Hakusan area tourism boards; filter out non-tourism OSM nodes (student housing) from hotel results.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines in Kii Peninsula; get_japan_heritage(Wakayama) returns 7 Japan Heritage stories. Wakanoura scenic site and the Saigoku pilgrimage story appear with detailed body text, themes, periods, source URLs. Kumano Kodo mentioned in body text. Excellent traceability with bunka.go.jp source URLs. Only limitation: mountain shrine entities themselves not surfaced as discrete items.
missing_entities: Nachi Taisha, Kumano Hongu Taisha as discrete spot entities (appear in heritage story text but not as individual items)
improvement_hint: Link Japan Heritage stories to constituent spot entities (shrines, temples) so agents can extract individual venue data from the story-level heritage records.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks along Sea of Japan coast; Niigata festivals include 長岡まつり大花火大会 (Nagaoka Grand Fireworks) — one of Japan's top 3 fireworks festivals, with accurate description (Phoenix, Sanshakudama), dates Aug 2-3. 129 total items. First two items are spring flower festivals but scrolling yields the key entity. Source URLs present.
missing_entities: Ojiya Fireworks Festival, Nagaoka fireworks is present but buried third in list
improvement_hint: Add event_subtype=fireworks tag to scraped events; rank fireworks events higher when query is summer fireworks; Nagaoka should be rank 1 for this query.

reasoning: Query asks for bamboo crafts in Oita; response returns 2 METI items: Hakata Ori (weaving, multi-prefecture including Oita) and 別府竹細工 (Beppu Bamboo Crafts) — precisely the primary entity expected. Accurate description of Madake bamboo, 8 techniques, production areas. Source URLs, designation dates correct. Only 2 items but they are exactly right.
improvement_hint: Consider adding bamboo craft experience venues (別府市竹細工伝統産業会館) to complement the METI designation data.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes in Shikoku; Ehime results return castles and historic buildings (Uwajima Castle, Matsuyama Castle, Ozu Castle, Uchiko-za). Shimanami Kaido — the world-famous cycling route in Ehime — not visible in the 4000-char truncation. Spots are accurate and well-structured but no cycling-specific entities.
missing_entities: Shimanami Kaido (しまなみ海道) cycling route — the most famous cycling route in Shikoku/Japan
improvement_hint: Add cycling_route as entity kinds; Shimanami Kaido has a Wikidata QID (Q855668) and should rank first for Ehime cycling queries.

reasoning: Query asks for kabuki outside Tokyo/Kyoto; get_traditional_arts returns 183 items. Kabuki (Q199701, UNESCO 2005) appears as second item. Bunraku ranked first (also relevant traditional art). Good UNESCO metadata with ids, inscription years, source URLs. QIDs present. Not geography-filtered so regional kabuki venues (Konpira kabuki, Uchiko-za) not surfaced as discrete spots.
missing_entities: Kotohira Kabuki (金丸座/金刀比羅宮), Uchiko-za regional kabuki — venue-level data missing
improvement_hint: Link traditional_arts entries to physical venue spots where regional performances occur; Konpira Kabuki (Kagawa) and Uchiko-za (Ehime) are canonical regional kabuki venues.
